info.name: Chat Service
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Chat Service (HRMIS_CHAT_SERVICE).

**Topology:**
- Chat service runs as `app.chat_main:app` on port **8517**
- Routed by API Gateway via Consul tag `path=/chat`
- All REST endpoints require JWT (`Authorization: Bearer <token>`)
- WebSocket uses query-string token: `?token=<jwt>`

**Setup:**
1. Set `gateway_url` to your gateway (e.g. `http://localhost:8050`) — this is the recommended path.
2. Set `auth_url` to your auth service (e.g. `http://localhost:8085`).
3. Run `0. Auth → Login` first — token is auto-saved to `{{token}}`.
4. (Optional) Set `chat_direct_url` if you want to bypass the gateway during dev.
5. Update `peer_user_id`, `team_id`, `conversation_id`, `message_id`, `attachment_id` after running corresponding requests.

**Endpoints covered:**
- Conversations: list, create DM, team, #general, get by id
- Messages: list, send (text/image/voice/file), reply, mark-read, edit, admin-delete, forward
- Attachments: upload, get pre-signed URL
- Presence: get user presence
- Search: full-text scoped to caller
- WebSocket: `/chat/ws` with sample client→server actions and expected server→client events

variable.description: API Gateway base URL (preferred for prod-like flows)
Direct chat service (bypass gateway, for local debugging)
WebSocket base. Use the gateway WS URL if your gateway proxies WS upgrades.
Set after a conversation is created
Set after a message is sent
Set after an attachment is uploaded

item.name: 0. Auth
item.description: Login through the Auth service to obtain a JWT. The collection scripts auto-save it to `{{token}}` and `{{user_id}}`.

request.description: Adjust path/body to match your auth service. The script auto-saves `access_token` → `{{token}}` and `user_id` → `{{user_id}}`.

item.name: 1. Conversations
item.description: Conversation creation + retrieval. The list endpoint returns the WhatsApp-style inbox with peer/team info, latest message preview, and unread count.

request.description:
Returns every conversation the caller belongs to, enriched with:
- `peer` (DM only): id, name, username, profile_image_key
- `team` (team only): id, name
- `latest_message`: id, sender_id, message_type, body_preview, created_at
- `unread_count`: integer

Sorted by `last_message_at DESC NULLS LAST`.

response.name: 200 OK example

request.description:
Idempotent: returns the existing DM if one already exists between caller and `peer_user_id`. Peer must be active (`enable=1`, not soft-deleted).

**Errors:**
- 403 `CHAT_USER_INACTIVE` — peer disabled
- 422 — peer_user_id missing/invalid

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Returns the team's chat. Lazy-creates on first access; reconciles members on subsequent calls.

**RBAC:** SuperAdmin/Admin → any team. Others → only teams they're in.

**Errors:** 403 `CHAT_TEAM_MEMBERSHIP_REQUIRED`.

response.name: 403 CHAT_TEAM_MEMBERSHIP_REQUIRED
response.body: {"error_code": "CHAT_TEAM_MEMBERSHIP_REQUIRED", "message": "You are not a member of this team"}

request.description: Singleton org-wide #general room. Auto-joins the caller as a member if not already.

Caller must be a member.

**Errors:** 403 `CHAT_NOT_MEMBER`, 404 `CHAT_NOT_FOUND`.

item.name: 2. Messages
item.description: Send / list / edit / delete / forward / mark-read.

query.description: opaque cursor from a previous response

request.description: Reverse-chronological. Use `next_cursor` from the response for the next (older) page.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
WhatsApp-style markers (`*bold*`, `_italic_`, `~strike~`, `` `code` ``) are preserved verbatim. `@username` mentions are auto-resolved and trigger high-priority notifications.

**Body limits:** 1–4000 chars.
Run `3. Attachments → Upload attachment` first to obtain the `attachment_id`. `body` is an optional caption.
Voice notes don't take a body. The attachment row carries `duration_seconds` and (optionally) a client-computed waveform. Max 5 minutes / 10 MB.
Generic file (PDF, doc, zip, etc.). Max 50 MB.
A reply is just a normal send with `reply_to_message_id`. The original message is referenced by id; clients render the quoted message above the reply.

Idempotent. Records that the caller has read this message.

- DM → publishes `message.read` to the sender (blue tick).
- Team / general → publishes `message.read_count` to all members.
- Always publishes `unread.update` to the caller's other tabs (cross-tab badge clear).

**Response:** 204 No Content.

Only the original sender, only `text` messages, only within 15 minutes of `created_at`. Original body is kept in `chat_message_edits`.

**Errors:**
- 403 `CHAT_EDIT_NOT_OWNER`
- 409 `CHAT_EDIT_WINDOW_EXPIRED`
- 410 `CHAT_MESSAGE_DELETED`

Soft-delete. Sets `deleted_at` and `deleted_by`. Body is preserved in DB for audit; subsequent fetches return `"[message deleted]"`.

**RBAC:** Caller's `role_name` must be `SuperAdmin` or `Admin`.

**Errors:** 403 `CHAT_ADMIN_ONLY`.

**Response:** 204 No Content.

Caller must be a member of every destination. The Admin override does NOT relax membership for forwarding.

**Errors:** 403 `CHAT_FORWARD_NOT_MEMBER`, 410 `CHAT_MESSAGE_DELETED`.

item.name: 3. Attachments
item.description: Two-step send: upload first → use the returned `attachment_id` when sending the message.

formdata.description: Pick a file: image, audio/webm, pdf, etc.

request.description:
Multipart upload. Server validates MIME (allow-list) and size (10 MB image/voice, 50 MB file) before pushing to S3.

**Errors:**
- 413 `CHAT_ATTACHMENT_TOO_LARGE`
- 415 `CHAT_ATTACHMENT_TYPE_NOT_ALLOWED`
Use after the URL embedded in the original message response has expired (default TTL 3600 s, memoised in-process for half that).
Returns `status` (`online` | `away` | `offline`) + `last_seen_at`. Always visible to anyone authenticated.

query.description: Required. FULLTEXT BOOLEAN MODE; falls back to LIKE %q%.

request.description: Scoped to conversations the caller is a member of. Excludes soft-deleted messages.

item.name: 6. WebSocket
item.description:
Single chat WebSocket for all real-time events. Postman v10+ recognises `ws://` URLs and opens them as WebSocket requests on import — if not, **right-click the request → 'Open in New Tab' → switch to WebSocket Request**, or **File → New → WebSocket Request** and paste the URL below.

**URL pattern:** `ws://<chat-host-or-gateway>/chat/ws?token=<jwt>`

**Heartbeat:** send `{"action": "ping"}` every 30 s. Server responds `{"type": "pong"}`. The Redis presence key TTL is 90 s — three missed pings = offline.

**Closure code 4001** = invalid token.

See the sample messages folder below for everything you can send / receive.

query.description: JWT issued by the auth service

request.description:
WebSocket endpoint. JWT goes in the query string because browsers can't set headers on `WebSocket()`. Cached in Redis for 60 s.

Once connected, the server:
1. Sets `chat:presence:{user_id}=online` (TTL 90 s)
2. Updates `chat_user_presence` row
3. Fans out `presence.update` to every co-conversation user

url.raw: (send this as a WebSocket text frame)

request.description:
**This is a TEMPLATE for the WebSocket frame to send — not an HTTP request.** Copy the JSON body and paste it into the 'Message' tab of the connected WebSocket request, then click Send.

Server replies: `{"type": "pong"}`.

url.raw: (send this as a WebSocket text frame)

request.description: Server fans out `typing.start` to other conversation members. Redis typing key TTL is 5 s, so silence = automatic stop.

url.raw: (send this as a WebSocket text frame)
(send this as a WebSocket text frame)

request.description:
Records read for caller. Triggers:
- `message.read` (DM, to sender)
- `message.read_count` (team / general, to all members)
- `unread.update` (caller's other tabs — cross-tab badge clear)

**The server pushes the following event types over the WebSocket. Format is always:**
```
{"type": "<name>", "data": { ... }}
```

## message.new
A new message in any conversation the user belongs to.
```
{"type":"message.new","data":{"conversation_id":42,"message":{"id":992,"sender_id":10,"message_type":"text","body":"hi","attachment":null,"reply_to_message_id":null,"forwarded_from_message_id":null,"edited_at":null,"deleted_at":null,"created_at":"2026-04-27T12:02:01","mentions":[]}}}
```

## message.edited
```
{"type":"message.edited","data":{"message_id":992,"conversation_id":42,"body":"updated","edited_at":"2026-04-27T12:05:30"}}
```

## message.deleted
```
{"type":"message.deleted","data":{"message_id":992,"conversation_id":42,"deleted_by":1}}
```

## message.read (DM only — sent to original sender)
```
{"type":"message.read","data":{"message_id":992,"user_id":11,"read_at":"2026-04-27T12:03:15"}}
```

## message.read_count (team/general — sent to all members)
```
{"type":"message.read_count","data":{"message_id":871,"conversation_id":17,"read_count":4}}
```

## message.delivered (reserved)
```
{"type":"message.delivered","data":{"message_id":992,"user_id":11,"delivered_at":"2026-04-27T12:02:02"}}
```

## typing.start / typing.stop
```
{"type":"typing.start","data":{"conversation_id":42,"user_id":11}}
```

## presence.update
```
{"type":"presence.update","data":{"user_id":11,"status":"online","last_seen_at":"2026-04-27T12:08:42"}}
```

## inbox.bump (drives the WhatsApp-style inbox cell)
Sent to every conversation member (sender included) when a new message lands.
```
{"type":"inbox.bump","data":{"conversation_id":42,"unread_count":2,"latest_message":{"id":992,"sender_id":11,"message_type":"text","body_preview":"see you tomorrow!","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:01:14","deleted_at":null}}}
```

## unread.update (cross-tab badge clear, sent to all of the user's WS connections)
```
{"type":"unread.update","data":{"conversation_id":42,"unread_count":0}}
```

## pong (reply to ping)
```
{"type":"pong"}
```
